title: chlorogalum divaricatum
intro: Chlorogalum divaricatum, also known as the white wild garlic or death camas, is a perennial plant native to the western coast of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues. Chlorogalum divaricatum can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and salves, although caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential for overuse.
